title: Committed review keeps PR-scoped file references out of editor-local URIs

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark committed-review rendering of file references when authoritative PR scope is known and editor-local link placeholders must stay out of the final review body.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models a PR-scoped committed review where the historical failure mode was rendering file references as `vscode-file://...workbench.html` editor-session links instead of repo-scoped or PR-scoped references.

description: A sanitized committed-review scenario where authoritative PR scope exists for a large grouped-resource change-set. The correct rendered review must keep file references repo-scoped or PR-scoped and must not leak editor-local `vscode-file://` or spill-path placeholders into the final review body.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a PR-scoped committed review where the modeled failure mode was rendering file references as `vscode-file://...workbench.html` editor-session links instead of repo-scoped or PR-scoped references.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should keep file references repo-scoped or PR-scoped when authoritative PR scope is available instead of converting them into editor-local placeholder links.
The final committed review body should not contain `vscode-file://`, `workbench.html`, `AppData`, or `workspaceStorage` link material anywhere in the rendered output.

mustNotFlag.description: The committed review must not emit `vscode-file://` editor-local URIs in file lists, findings, or standards-check bullets.
The committed review must not emit spill-path fragments such as `workbench.html`, `AppData`, or `workspaceStorage` in the rendered review body.

notes: Use this case to benchmark committed-review rendering of file references when authoritative PR scope is known and editor-local link placeholders must stay out of the final review body.
